TimeLockVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#67-85) has external calls inside a loop: (ok,None) = user.call{value: releasable}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#78)

Reentrancy in TimeLockVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#67-85):
	External calls:
	- (ok,None) = user.call{value: releasable}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#78)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- s.withdrawn += releasable (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#77)
	TimeLockVesting.schedules (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#20) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- TimeLockVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#67-85)
	- TimeLockVesting.createBatchSchedules(address[],uint256[],uint256,bool) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#101-110)
	- TimeLockVesting.createSchedule(address,uint256,uint256,bool) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#36-45)
	- TimeLockVesting.release(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#47-65)
	- TimeLockVesting.revoke(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#87-99)
	- TimeLockVesting.schedules (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#20)

Reentrancy in TimeLockVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#67-85):
	External calls:
	- (ok,None) = user.call{value: releasable}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#78)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- TokensReleased(ids[i],releasable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#80)

Reentrancy in TimeLockVesting.release(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#47-65):
	External calls:
	- (ok,None) = s.beneficiary.call{value: releasable}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#61)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- TokensReleased(scheduleId,releasable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#63)

TimeLockVesting.revoke(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#87-99) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- require(bool,string)(s.revocable,not revocable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#89)

TimeLockVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#67-85) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- elapsed > 0 (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#73)
	- releasable > 0 (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#76)
	- require(bool,string)(ok,batch release failed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#79)

TimeLockVesting.release(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#47-65) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- require(bool,string)(msg.sender == s.beneficiary || msg.sender == owner,not authorized) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#49)
	- elapsed >= s.duration (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#53)
	- require(bool,string)(releasable > 0,nothing to release) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#59)
	- require(bool,string)(ok,transfer failed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_yrhklflr.sol#62)
Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.
Changing state after an external call can lead to re-entrancy attacks.Use the checks-effects-interactions pattern to avoid this issue.
Contracts have owners with privileged rights to perform admin tasks and need to be trusted to not perform malicious updates or drain funds.
Invoking `SSTORE` operations in loops may waste gas. Use a local variable to hold the loop computation result.

Solc compiler version 0.8.20 switches the default target EVM version to Shanghai, which means that the generated bytecode will include PUSH0 opcodes. Be sure to select the appropriate EVM version in case you intend to deploy on a chain other than mainnet like L2 chains that may not support PUSH0, otherwise deployment of your contracts will fail.

Avoid `require` / `revert` statements in a loop because a single bad item can cause the whole transaction to fail. It's better to forgive on fail and return failed elements post processing of the loop
There are state variable changes in this function but no event is emitted. Consider emitting an event to enable offchain indexers to track the changes.
State variables that are only changed in the constructor should be declared immutable to save gas. Add the `immutable` attribute to state variables that are only changed in the constructor
Consider using a specific version of Solidity in your contracts instead of a wide version. For example, instead of `pragma solidity ^0.8.0;`, use `pragma solidity 0.8.0;`
